and atlas clusters commands for cloud deployments. We updated
this guide to use the new commands. In this guide, you will learn how to create a MongoDB Atlas

structured_data.method: Structured data quality and completeness validation
structured_data.score_calculation: Type appropriateness (20) + Field completeness (30) + Multiple formats (20) + Schema validation (30)

steps.text:
Before you begin this tutorial, you must install
the following dependencies in your development
environment:Atlas CLI: Command-line interface that allows you to manage
your deployments from the terminalMongoDB Shell: Interactive tool that connects to a deployment and
provides database operation supportDocker: Platform that allows you to run software within containers,
including local MongoDB deploymentsSelect the tab corresponding to your operating system
to view the commands that install these required development
tools.Run the following commands to install the dependencies
by using the Homebrew package manager. If you do not have
Homebrew, you can install it by following the instructions on the
Homebrew website.Run the following commands to install the dependencies
by using the Chocolatey package manager. If you do not
have Chocolatey, you can install it by following the instructions
on the Chocolatey website.For other ways to install the Atlas CLI, see Atlas
CLI install page.The preceding commands install the Docker Desktop
application. After the installation completes, ensure that you create
a Docker account and start the application.
steps.name: Install dependencies

steps.text:
Run the following command and follow the prompts in the shell to deploy a cluster.
If you do not have an Atlas account, the following command
prompts you to create one.This command creates a single-member replica set in a container
that runs on your local machine.Replace the <port number> placeholder with the port
you want to use. The default port is 27017, but you
can specify a different port if 27017 is not available.The command outputs the following information:Save the connection string URI for use in a future step.This command creates a free tier cluster on MongoDB Atlas.Replace the following placeholder values to create a new
database user with
atlasAdmin privileges in your deployment:<database username>: Specify a username for your new database user<database user password>: Specify a password for your new database userThe command outputs the following information:Save the connection string URI for use in a future step.
steps.name: Set up a fully-featured deployment

steps.text:
You can connect to your deployment with the MongoDB Shell (mongosh) by running the
following command:Replace the <database username> and <database user password> placeholders
with the username and password you created for your database user.After connecting, you can run the following command to test
your connection:The command returns a list of databases in your deployment.
steps.name: Connect to your deployment

dom_navigability.score_calculation: Based on violation severity and frequency

metadata_completeness.method: Metadata field presence and quality check
metadata_completeness.score_calculation: Sum of field scores (title 15, description 15, author 15, date 15, topic 15, language 10, canonical 15)

http_compliance.score_calculation: HTML reachability (15) + Redirect efficiency (25) + Robots/crawl permissions (20) + Cache headers (20) + Agent content hints (20)

agent_content_hints.rationale: PRD F2.2/F2.3: retrieval-lift evidence deferred to Phase 6

caveats: Single corpus, single grader architecture, single run.
Four pillars carry zero headline weight in v2; still reported as diagnostics.
Profile-specific weights collapse to the same 2-pillar composite pending corpus-003.

Cross-judge accuracy variance: per-judge corpus-002 means span 0.591-0.698 across Llama-3.3-70B, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-V3.2 (pooled kappa 0.706-0.817). The v2 composite-vs-accuracy Pearson r ranges from +0.440 to +0.618 across judges (all above the +0.35 ship gate). Report majority-vote CI [0.567, 0.688] or per-judge union CI [0.530, 0.758] when comparing across studies.
